dataset: ESA/ESO/NASA FITS Liberator M12 Educational Dataset

pi_items.source: Wikipedia, SIMBAD, NASA Messier Catalog, multiple astronomical databases
SIMBAD, Harris (2010) globular cluster catalog, Wikipedia

pi_items.verified_value: Vcomb.fits: V-band combined VLT image, 2048x2048 pixels, float32, mean pixel value ~20.45
pi_items.source: ESA Hubble FITS Liberator M12 data page (esahubble.org/projects/fits_liberator/m12data/); verified by direct FITS inspection in live environment

pi_items.verified_value: Bcomb.fits: B-band combined VLT image, 2048x2048 pixels, float32, 16.8 MB
pi_items.source: ESA Hubble FITS Liberator M12 data page; verified by direct FITS inspection in live environment

pi_items.verified_value: Generated at task setup from m12_B_V.xls (original ESA file). The original XLS has 3 columns only (B, V, B-V) with NO pixel coordinates. Setup script detects stars from FITS images and matches by magnitude to assign pixel positions. Final CSV has columns: star_id, x_pixel, y_pixel, V_mag, B_mag, BV_color
pi_items.source: Direct inspection of m12_B_V.xls in live VM using xlrd; file command reports 'Composite Document File V2, Author: Lars Lindberg Christensen, Created 2001-08-23'

pi_items.verified_value: Original XLS: 170 data rows + 1 header = 171 total rows. After position-matching to FITS detections: 169 stars successfully matched. Task metadata claim of 171 is the total XLS row count (slightly misleading — actual usable stars: 169)
pi_items.source: Direct xlrd inspection in live VM: ws.nrows=171, ws.ncols=3; matching result: 169/170 stars matched

pi_items.verified_value: Actual catalog minimum B-V = 0.647. The claim of 0.5 is a lower bound that allows margin, so it is reasonable as a verifier threshold
pi_items.source: Direct computation from parsed catalog in live VM

pi_items.verified_value: Actual catalog maximum B-V = 0.986. The claim of 1.1 is an upper bound with margin, reasonable as a verifier threshold
pi_items.source: Direct computation from parsed catalog in live VM

pi_items.verified_value: 8 pixels. Reasonable for a 2048x2048 VLT image. Typical stellar FWHM in these images is ~3-5 pixels, so aperture radius of 8 (~2x FWHM) is standard practice
pi_items.source: Standard aperture photometry practice; consistent with setup script detection parameters

pi_items.verified_value: 15 pixels for inner sky annulus. Standard practice: sky annulus starts at ~2x aperture radius
25 pixels for outer sky annulus. Standard practice: annulus width of 10 pixels is typical
Data taken with ESO's Very Large Telescope (VLT), distributed as educational material via ESA/ESO/NASA FITS Liberator project. Created by Lars Lindberg Christensen (ESA) in 2001
pi_items.source: ESA Hubble FITS Liberator M12 page (esahubble.org/projects/fits_liberator/m12data/); XLS file metadata shows 'Author: Lars Lindberg Christensen, Created: 2001-08-23'

pi_items.verified_value: URL is active. Individual files: Vcomb.zip, Bcomb.zip, m12_B_V.xls are downloadable from this base URL
pi_items.source: ESA Hubble website (esahubble.org/projects/fits_liberator/m12data/); install_astroimagej.sh downloads from this URL

pi_items.verified_value: Brightest star in catalog has V = 15.2043 (original XLS). After position-matching, brightest matched star is V = 15.6809. The discrepancy is because the brightest XLS star (V=15.2043) was not matched to a detected image source within 0.5 mag tolerance
pi_items.source: Direct inspection of m12_B_V.xls first data row: B=16.2166, V=15.2043; catalog CSV brightest: V=15.6809

pi_items.verified_value: 1.5 magnitude tolerance is generous. Given the ZP uncertainty and the V=15.20-15.68 range of the brightest stars, this tolerance should accommodate correct measurements
pi_items.source: Analysis of catalog data spread and typical photometric uncertainties

pi_items.verified_value: 20 stars is achievable. The catalog has 169 matched stars. An agent placing apertures on bright, well-separated stars should easily find 20+. However, manual GUI aperture placement on 20 stars requires ~40-60 mouse clicks (2-3 per star) in each band
pi_items.source: Analysis of catalog density and AstroImageJ workflow requirements

privileged_info_summary: Messier 12 (NGC 6218) is a globular cluster in Ophiuchus at distance ~16,400 light-years, with metallicity [Fe/H] = -1.37 and reddening E(B-V) = 0.19. The VLT images (Vcomb.fits V-band, Bcomb.fits B-band, both 2048x2048 float32) and star catalog are real observational data from the ESA/ESO/NASA FITS Liberator educational project. The reference catalog (m12_catalog.csv) contains 169 stars with pixel positions and published B/V magnitudes. Stars span V = 15.68 to 20.43 and B-V = 0.65 to 0.99, covering the red giant branch and upper main sequence — no horizontal branch or blue stars are present. The photometric zero-point between instrumental and catalog V magnitudes is approximately ZP_V = 31.07. The agent must perform aperture photometry (radius 8, sky annulus 15-25) on at least 20 stars in BOTH bands, write a Python script to cross-match stars between bands and against the catalog, compute zero-points, create a calibrated CMD plot (V vs B-V), and report the zero-points and brightest calibrated V magnitude.
